html:
• Understanding any complex system becomes easier when we break it into smaller parts.
  • Each part should be studied independently before combining it with others.
  • Clarity comes from structure, not from memorization.
  • A well-structured idea reduces confusion at every step.
  • We should always identify the core components first.
  • Then we analyze how these components interact with each other.
  • This interaction gives rise to observable behavior.
  • Once behavior is understood, prediction becomes possible.
  • Prediction is a sign of deep understanding.
  • Finally, the system becomes intuitive rather than mechanical.
  • Learning often fails when we try to memorize without understanding.
  • Memorization creates fragile knowledge that breaks under pressure.
  • A better approach is to build concepts step by step.
  • At each step, only one idea should be in focus.
  • All other information should fade into the background.
  • This reduces cognitive overload significantly.
  • Focused attention improves retention and clarity.
  • Gradual exposure helps build confidence in learning.
  • Confidence allows deeper exploration of ideas.
  • Ultimately, learning becomes a structured experience.
